title: dalea humilis
intro: Dalea humilis, also known as the low prairie rose or little barb, is a perennial plant native to the prairies of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems, due to its ability to promote healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Dalea humilis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
